# README: remove centralized-SPOF undersell, elevate v13 cloud-on-your-codebase, strengthen compelling "why try it"

## Context

Follow-up to the README apex/presentation rollout (Epic #12225; #12251 / PR #12252 merged). Operator review of the merged README on `dev` (2026-05-31) flagged three issues — the first an undersell correction, the latter two a compellingness gap.

## The Problems (operator-flagged)

1. **The "Honest Caveat: A Centralized Brain" section is false / underselling.** It frames the Brain as running "on a single operator's infrastructure… a Single Point of Failure… not yet immortal." Operator: *"we have cloud deployments ready… 'runs on a single machine' is NOT true… I can easily backup the graph data to a different machine or apple time capsule."* The Brain is cloud-deployable (v13 multi-tenant) and its entire state is portable files (SQLite Native Edge Graph + vector store). This is the recurring undersell pattern — framing current reality as a fragility.
2. **v13 multi-tenant cloud reads as a side note.** The "deploy the Agent OS on your own codebase" capability — one of the strongest reasons to try Neo.mjs — is buried as a mid-document section with a version-led headline. Operator: *"cloud => v13 multi tenant item is a side note."*
3. **Few compelling arguments for *why to try* Neo.mjs.** The README is strong at *what Neo is* and *who it's for* (the "Who This Is For" filter is strong) but thin on *what you walk away with*. Operator: *"there are little compelling arguments on why to even try out neo."*

## The Fix

1. **Delete** `## The Honest Caveat: A Centralized Brain`. Fold a *resilience-as-strength* statement into the v13 section: Body distributed (every clone a backup); Brain state = portable files (snapshot/back-up/redeploy anywhere); v13 runs it as a cloud deployment, not one operator's box. Keep #10291 referenced as forward-hardening (self-defense + sustainable funding), not present fragility.
2. **Elevate + rewrite v13.** Move the section up (after the hero, before the deep `## The Two Hemispheres`), rename to a benefit headline ("Deploy a Cross-Model AI Engineering Team on Your Own Codebase"), and strengthen it into a compelling pitch (persistent memory + cross-family review + self-improvement on *your* repo; "a config entry, not a fork").
3. **Strengthen compelling "why try it" arguments** — lead the Body with the engineer outcome (40k+ ops/sec without jank; multi-window object permanence; zero build/zero deps) and surface the proof-of-life (the swarm already runs its own lifecycle: 706 merged PRs, cross-family review).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `## The Honest Caveat: A Centralized Brain` removed; no "single machine / SPOF / not yet immortal" framing remains.
- [ ] Resilience reframed as strength (distributed Body + portable/cloud-deployable Brain) within the v13 section; #10291 referenced as forward-hardening only.
- [ ] v13 section moved above `## The Two Hemispheres`, benefit-headlined, and strengthened into a compelling deploy-on-your-codebase pitch.
- [ ] At least one strengthened compelling "why try it" argument per audience (engineer outcome + proof-of-life).
- [ ] No client name (capability framing only); no framework-negation anti-pattern (`grep framework|React|Angular|Vue` → 0); all relative links resolve; hygiene clean.
- [ ] Operator wording-eyes (front-page taste) + cross-family review (@neo-gpt) before merge.

## Out of Scope

- The two-hemispheres structure, MX-loop block + motto, Institution table (keep — operator-confirmed).
- Other identity surfaces (VISION, llms.txt — separate merged subs).
- SEO priority map (#12255 — @neo-gpt's lane).
